title: Polymeric polyoxazolines
abstract: Polymeric polyoxazolines of the following structure are disclosed (I), where X is the residue of a polymeric polyol after reaction with a dicarboxylic acid/anhydride; where R 5 is an organic moiety; where Y is an organic moiety containing one or more oxazoline groups; and wherein each R 10 is independently hydrogen or an organic moiety. Methods of making such polyoxazolines and using them in a coating, and packages coated therewith, are also within the scope of the present invention.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A polymeric polyoxazoline having the following structure: Rio Rio Rio Rio â Yâ câ Nâ C - câ oâ câ R 5 â câ oâ Xâ oâ câ R 5 -Câ oâ C - câ N II I I I II II II II I I I O H R 10 Rio 0 0 0 0 Rio R10 H where X is the residue of a polymeric polyol after reaction with a dicarboxylic acid/anhydride; where Rs is an organic moiety; where Y is an organic moiety containing one or more oxazoline groups; and wherein each Rio is independently hydrogen or an organic moiety. 2. The polymeric polyoxazoline of claim 1 in which X is the residue of a polyether polyol. 3. The polymeric polyoxazoline of claim 1 in which each Y independently contains one or two oxazoline groups. 4. The polymeric polyoxazoline of claim 1 wherein Rs is C2H4 or ObHio. 5. The polymeric polyoxazoline of claim 1 wherein R10 is hydrogen. 6. A method for making the polymeric polyoxazoline of claim 1 comprising: (a) reacting a polymeric polyol having terminal and/or pendant hydroxyl groups with a dicarboxylic acid/anhydride to form a carboxylic acid functional polymer, and (b) reacting the carboxylic functional polymer with a polyoxazoline containing at least two oxazoline groups. 7. The method of claim 6 in which the polymeric polyol comprises a polyester polyol, a polyether polyol, a polyurethane polyol, or mixtures thereof. 8. The method of claim 6 in which the dicarboxylic acid/anhydride is an anhydride containing 2 to 30 carbon atoms. 9. The method of claim 8 in which the anhydride comprises hexahydroxl phthalic anhydride. 10. The method of claim 7 in which the polymeric polyol comprises a polyether polyol. 11. The method of claim 6 in which the polyoxazoline comprises a phenylene-bis- oxazoline. 12. The method of claim 11 in which the phenylene-bis-oxazoline comprises 1 ,3- phenylene-bis-oxazoline. 13. The method of claim 6 in which the polyoxazoline comprises 2,2',2"-(hexane- 1 ,3,6-triyl) tris(4,5-dihydrooxazole). 14. A coating composition comprising: (a) a polymer comprising acidic functionality, and (b) a polymeric polyoxazoline crosslinking agent having the following structure: Rio Rio Rio Rio O I 10 II â Yâ câ Nâ câ câ oâ câ R 5 â Câ Oâ xâ oâ câ R 5 -Câ Oâ Câ -câ Nâ câ Yâ II I I I II II II II I I I O H R-io Rio 0O 0O 0 O 0 O R-io Ri 0 H 

 where X is the residue of a polymeric polyol after reaction with a dicarboxylic acid/anhydride; where R 5 is an organic moiety; where Y is an organic moiety containing one or more oxazoline groups; and wherein each R 10 is independently hydrogen or an organic moiety. 15. The coating composition of claim 14 in which the polymer comprising acidic functionality comprises a polyester polymer and/or a (meth)acrylic polymer. 16. The coating composition of claim 14 in which (a) and (b) are dispersed in aqueous medium. 17. The coating composition of claim 14 in which the molar ratio of polyoxazoline to carboxylic acid is 0.05:1 to 2:1. 18. A package comprising a coating composition applied to at least a portion of a surface of the package, the coating composition comprising: (a) a polymer comprising acidic functionality, and (b) a polymeric polyoxazoline crosslinking agent having the following structure: where X is the residue of a polymeric polyol after reaction with a dicarboxylic acid/anhydride; where R 5 is an organic moiety; where Y is an organic moiety containing one or more oxazoline groups; and wherein each R 10 is independently hydrogen or an organic moiety. 19. The package of claim 18 in which the package is a metal can. 20. The package of claim 18 in which the coating is applied to a food-contacting surface of the package. 21 . The package of claim 19 in which the coating composition is applied to the surface of a can end. 22. The package of claim 18 in which the coating composition is an aqueous-based composition and (a) and (b) are dispersed in aqueous medium. 23. The package of claim 18 in which the polymer comprising acidic functionality comprises a carboxyl group-containing (meth)acrylic polymer, a carboxyl group- containing polyester polymer, or mixtures thereof. 24. The coating composition of claim 14, wherein the composition is substantially free of formaldehyde. 25. The coating composition of claim 14, wherein the composition is substantially free of BPA. 26. A method of coating a package comprising applying to at least a portion of the package the coating of Claim 14. 27. The method of Claim 8 in which the acid comprises adipic acid.
